text.title: AI Content Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every AI Content Strategy application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: AI handles research, outlines, drafts, SEO metadata, repurposing variants. Humans handle strategy, expert insights, proprietary data, brand voice, fact verification.
rule.rationale: AI aggregates existing web content; humans supply what AI cannot generate. Combination is what Google rewards.

rule.statement: Every AI draft requires human review and enrichment before scheduling. Automated pipelines must include a hard human-approval gate before publish.
rule.rationale: AI pipelines outpace human review; without a hard gate, defective content publishes at scale.

rule.statement: Every published piece includes ≥2 of: owned research/data, first-hand experience, expert quote, specific case study, contrarian opinion with evidence.
rule.rationale: Generic content without first-hand experience is algorithmically deprioritised.

rule.statement: Brand voice guide is injected as a system prompt in every AI generation call.
rule.rationale: Independent AI sessions without the guide drift to generic marketing voice.

rule.statement: Repurposing restructures for channel conventions; never trim-from-original. Twitter ≠ trimmed blog post.
rule.rationale: Format-swap repurposing loses channel-native feel and underperforms.

rule.statement: Every quantitative claim in AI-generated content is verified against a primary source before publish.
rule.rationale: AI fabricates plausible statistics; primary-source verification is the only defence.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
